title: Photochemical reactor type device
abstract: The invention concerns the field of devices allowing photochemical reactions. The invention is more particularly concerned with a device for carrying out a photoreaction at a regulated or controlled temperature, in particular at very low temperatures.
claims:
Claims 1. Device for carrying out a photoreaction at a regulated or controlled temperature, lower than 10Â°C or higher than 30Â°C, and comprising: â¢a tank (1), having an upper surface (la) and a lower surface (lb), being capable of containing a heating or cooling means (3), optionally in the presence of a temperature control means, â¢at least one reaction unit (2), â¢at least one light source (4) whose light rays are capable of illuminating the said reaction unit (2), o the said at least one reaction unit (2): -being contained in the tank (1) -being arranged to comprise at least one container (6), comprising an upper part (6a) and a lower part (6b), capable of containing a reaction medium (7), -and comprising an enclosure (8) having an upper part (8a) and a lower part (8b) surrounding the at least one container (6), at least in its longitudinal axis, and in contact with it, provided that at least one of the upper (6a) and lower (6b) parts of the said container (6) can be traversed at least in part by the light rays from the said at least one light source (4), the said at least one light source (4) being positioned either inside the container in the longitudinal axis of the enclosure in its upper or lower part, preferably between parts 6a-8a and 6b-8b, or outside the container, in the longitudinal axis of the enclosure and in such a way that its light rays are directed at least partially onto at least one of the upper (6a) and lower (6b) parts of the said container (6), allowing the reaction medium (7) to be illuminated when it is present in the said container (6). 2. A device according to claim 1, wherein the tank (1) is made of a material resistant to temperatures ranging from -100Â°C to 120Â°C. 3. A device according to claim 2, wherein the container comprises either a single wall of plastic material or a single wall of metal. 4. A device according to any of claims 1 to 3, wherein the tank (1) is surrounded by an insulating layer (9) inserted between the inner and outer walls of the tank (1) or placed outside the tank (1). 5. Device according to any of claims 1 to 4, wherein the refrigeration means (3) is at least one refrigerant solid or refrigerant fluid in liquid or gaseous phase or a cryogenic fluid cooled by an external cryostat. 6. Device according to any of claims 1 to 5, wherein the reaction unit (2) consists of at least one container (6) capable of containing a reaction medium (7) and an enclosure (8) in contact with the at least one container (6). 7. Device according to any of claims 1 to 6, wherein the said enclosure (8) of the reaction unit (2) is made of metal, in particular copper, aluminium or brass, preferably brass. 8. A device according to any of claims 1 to 7, wherein the said enclosure (8) of the reaction unit (2) is a metal column passing through the lower surface (lb) of the tank (1) or being fixed/welded to the lower surface (lb) of the tank (1). 9. Device according to any of claims 1 to 8, in which the light source (4) is placed in the enclosure (8) under the at least one container (6) between the lower part of the tube (6b) and the lower part (lb) of the tank (1). 10. A process for carrying out a photochemical reaction at low temperature, preferably at a temperature ranging from -100Â°C to 10Â°C, using a device according to any of claims 1 to 9, which consists of : a) placing a reaction medium in a container (6), 

 b) placing said container (6) containing the reaction medium in a metal enclosure (8), said enclosure being placed in a tank in contact with a cooling means, and said container being in contact with said enclosure (8), (c) placing a light source (4) above or below the container (6), (d) carrying out the photochemical reaction, (e) recovering the product(s) of the photochemical reaction.
